qualifying_org_definition: Indiana does not have a general volunteer-at-nonprofit lane for ABAWD compliance. The four documented compliance paths in Chapter 2400 Section 2438.17.10 are: (1) paid work averaging 20 hrs/week (80 hrs/month); (2) participation in an approved SNAP E&T activity through IMPACT, WIOA, TAA, or 'any other approved employment and training activity' for 20 hrs/week; (3) combination of work and approved E&T for 20 hrs/week; (4) Workfare/CWEP for the allotment-formula number of hours. SNAP E&T activities (Chapter 2500 Section 2540.10.00) are: Supervised Job Search, Job Readiness, Work Experience Program (WE), CWEP/Workfare, Vocational Training, Educational/Academic, and Job Retention Services. 'Community Service Programs' (Section 2540.10.40) is explicitly TANF-only and requires a Community Service Agreement with the IMPACT office plus daily supervision. SNAP CWEP is limited to public and private nonprofit employers but must be set up and supervised by IMPACT staff with a formal worksite agreement — it is not independent volunteering. The 'volunteer work (except VISTA or AmeriCorps)' language in Section 2438.15.05 explicitly states such work does not exempt individuals from work registration (this is in the exemption context, not an ABAWD compliance path). VISTA and AmeriCorps are the only named volunteer programs that count. The 'any other approved employment and training activity' path requires IMPACT approval and case manager oversight, not unilateral documentation by the recipient.
verification_instrument: No named Indiana-specific volunteer verification form exists. ABAWD compliance is documented through the IMPACT System of Record (SOR) for IMPACT-assigned activities. For CWEP, the RECORD OF IMPACT ATTENDANCE form (State Form 44720) is used — submitted to the IMPACT case manager at least monthly. For paid employment, employer-produced documentation is required. There is no standalone verification pathway for independent nonprofit volunteering because Indiana does not recognize that as a qualifying ABAWD compliance activity.
hours_formula_detail: Indiana follows the federal 80 hrs/month (20 hrs/week) threshold for work, paid employment, and approved E&T paths. CWEP/Workfare path uses the allotment-over-minimum-wage formula per 7 CFR 273.24, which typically yields fewer than 80 hours for most benefit levels. The 80-hour figure is the primary quoted threshold in Section 2438.17.10.
accepts_outofstate_detail: Indiana has no general self-service volunteer lane — so the out-of-state nonprofit question does not arise for the primary compliance pathway. For the CWEP path (the only nonprofit-adjacent lane), the nonprofit must execute a formal worksite agreement with an Indiana IMPACT office, which is a de facto in-state administrative requirement (the worksite must be physically accessible to IMPACT staff for daily supervision). State policy (Chapter 2500) is silent on the geographic location of 501(c)(3) status; it requires IMPACT-approved worksite agreements, not that the org be incorporated in Indiana. No state policy language explicitly bars or permits out-of-state nonprofits from holding CWEP worksite agreements.
geographic_waivers: none — fully enforced statewide. Indiana has not held a statewide or county-level ABAWD waiver. As of 2026, no Indiana counties appear on the FNA active-waiver list. OBBBA further tightened waiver criteria (10% unemployment threshold), making future waivers unlikely for Indiana's labor market.
submission_deadline: RECORD OF IMPACT ATTENDANCE form submitted to IMPACT case manager at least monthly; no specific calendar day cutoff found in published policy. Recorded in IMPACT System of Record (SOR) by case manager.
notes:
Indiana's SNAP policy manual (Chapters 2400 and 2500, FSSA/DFR) does not articulate a general volunteer lane for ABAWD compliance independent of the IMPACT E&T program. Section 2438.17.10 lists four compliance paths — paid work (20 hrs/week), approved E&T (IMPACT/WIOA/TAA or 'any other approved employment and training activity'), combination of work+E&T, and CWEP/Workfare — but does not define standalone volunteer work at a nonprofit as a qualifying path, and 'Community Service Programs' (Section 2540.10.40) require a Community Service Agreement between IMPACT and the provider and are listed only under TANF (not SNAP) activities. Indiana also explicitly states that 'volunteer work (except VISTA or AmeriCorps)' does not qualify as 'gainfully employed' for work registration exemption purposes (though this is in the exemption context, not ABAWD compliance). No named state verification form for volunteer hours was found. The lane is classified Tier 3 because the general nonprofit volunteer path cannot be confirmed from published state policy; the state relies on IMPACT-mediated E&T as the documented mechanism. Medium confidence because state policy may implement the federal general lane administratively without publishing it in the policy manual.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Conducted four new search angles not used in the first pass: (1) Indiana SNAP E&T State Plan FY2026/volunteer community service; (2) IMPACT program SNAP ABAWD community service 2025-2026; (3) Indiana food assistance ABAWD employment plan/case manager requirement; (4) Indiana SNAP approved volunteer site lists and verification forms. Key findings: Multiple secondary sources (WIOA state plan summaries, SNAP screener aggregators, FSSA own page language) confirm that volunteer work at a nonprofit for 20+ hours/week (80+ hours/month) satisfies Indiana's ABAWD work requirement under the federal work-equivalency rule — Indiana is applying 7 CFR 273.24(a)(2)(iii) even though the state policy manual does not explicitly label it as a 'general volunteer lane.' The critical finding on required_prestep: Indiana policy explicitly states 'For SNAP, participation in IMPACT is voluntary' — the IMPACT auto-scheduled appointment is a referral mechanism, not a legal prerequisite to having volunteer hours count. ABAWDs who are already meeting the 80-hour threshold through independent volunteer work can demonstrate compliance without going through IMPACT at all. No evidence was found of an Indiana-maintained pre-approved volunteer site list or a state MOU/agreement requirement for nonprofit hosts in the general SNAP lane (the Community Service Agreement requirement is limited to TANF/IMPACT activities). No named Indiana volunteer verification form was found in the DFR forms library; verification appears to use the federal default (caseworker review of third-party documentation or signed statement). No county-level variation was found — administration is statewide through FSSA/DFR. The tier is revised from 3_blocked_or_unclear to 2_hoops because: the general volunteer lane is confirmed to exist and IMPACT enrollment is not a mandatory prestep, but the state policy manual does NOT explicitly articulate this lane (creating ambiguity), and the verification instrument is not a named form (caseworker discretion). The lane is accessible but requires navigating the IMPACT auto-referral system and providing adequate documentation to a caseworker in the absence of a standardized state form. Confidence remains medium because: the confirmation of the volunteer lane comes from secondary sources and WIOA plan summaries rather than a direct readable cite to the state policy manual text itself (the PDFs were binary-encoded and unreadable via web fetch), and the practical administrative pathway for documenting general nonprofit volunteer hours is not described in any published Indiana form or plain-language guide.

FINAL PASS (2026-06-21): Successfully extracted full text from both Indiana policy manual PDFs (Chapter 2400, 2.8 MB; Chapter 2500, 588 KB) using pypdf — both downloaded and read cleanly from in.gov. This resolves the prior 'binary-unreadable' barrier. Key findings from primary source text: (1) Section 2438.17.10 (COMPLIANCE WITH ABAWD) lists exactly four paths: paid work, IMPACT/WIOA/TAA/'any other approved E&T activity,' combination work+E&T, and CWEP/Workfare. No general volunteer-at-nonprofit lane is listed or implied. (2) Section 2540.10.00 (TANF AND SNAP IMPACT ACTIVITIES) lists SNAP E&T activities as: Supervised Job Search, Job Readiness, Work Experience (WE), CWEP/Workfare, Vocational Training, Educational, and Job Retention. 'Community Service Programs' appears only in the TANF column — confirmed excluded from SNAP. (3) Section 2540.10.40 (COMMUNITY SERVICE PROGRAMS) is explicitly marked '(C)' (TANF/Cash assistance), not '(S)' (SNAP). It requires a Community Service Agreement with the IMPACT Office and daily supervision. (4) Section 2540.10.30 (CWEP) confirms SNAP recipients can perform CWEP work at 'public and private non-profit employers' — but this requires IMPACT office site setup, formal worksite agreement, daily supervision, and weekly progress reports. This is not independent volunteering. (5) Section 2438.15.05 (EXEMPTIONS FROM WORK REGISTRATION) explicitly states 'Individuals engaged in hobbies or volunteer work (except VISTA or AmeriCorps)... cannot be considered gainfully employed and may not be exempt from Work Registration.' This confirms that Indiana treats general volunteering as a non-qualifying activity. (6) The 'any other approved employment and training activity' language in 2438.17.10 is the federal hook — but 'approved' means IMPACT/DFR-sanctioned with case manager oversight and SOR recording, not self-initiated nonprofit work. Revised conclusions: general_volunteer_lane = 'no' (confirmed from primary source); required_prestep = 'assignment' (IMPACT assignment to an approved E&T activity is required; SNAP CWEP at nonprofits requires IMPACT site setup and formal agreement); hours_reporting = 'county_determined' (hours recorded by IMPACT case manager in SOR or via CWEP attendance forms, not self-reported); remote_stance = 'restricted' (CWEP and WE require daily in-person supervision at worksite; supervised job search requires in-person at state-approved locations); tier = '3_blocked_or_unclear' (reinstated: there is no documented general volunteer lane — the pathway that exists for nonprofits is CWEP, which requires IMPACT administrative setup and is not independently accessible); confidence = 'high' (primary policy source documents read in full; findings are unambiguous and consistent across both chapters; prior 'medium' confidence was entirely due to inability to read the PDFs). The prior pass secondary sources that suggested a 'general volunteer lane' appear to have been misinterpreting the federal 7 CFR 273.24(a)(2)(iii) default and applying it to Indiana without verifying Indiana's actual implementation — Indiana channels ABAWD compliance through IMPACT, not a self-service volunteer lane.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): Added five new dimension groups. (1) Hours formula: Indiana quotes 80 hrs/month / 20 hrs/week throughout Chapter 2400; CWEP uses allotment-formula (workfare) but the canonical ABAWD threshold is federal_80. monthly_hours_required=80, hours_formula='federal_80'. (2) Out-of-state nonprofit: Not directly addressed in state policy because there is no general volunteer lane. CWEP worksite agreements require IMPACT office coordination and physical worksite supervision, which is a de facto in-state operational requirement, but no explicit in-state incorporation or 501(c)(3) location rule was found. Set to 'silent' with explanatory detail. (3) Geographic waivers: Indiana has no active ABAWD waivers and never has held a county-level waiver. FNA waiver list confirms no Indiana entries for FY2025-2029. Set to 'none — fully enforced statewide.' (4) Submission UX: RECORD OF IMPACT ATTENDANCE (State Form 44720) submitted to IMPACT case manager at least monthly, recorded in SOR. Mechanism is paper to county IMPACT office; no online portal found for ABAWD hour submission. Cadence=monthly. (5) Form fields: State Form 44720 fields identified from search results and policy text: facility/site (org_name), participant name (recipient_name), case number (recipient_case_number), instructor/supervisor name (supervisor_name), daily hours by day of week (hours_by_day_or_week), week beginning date (period_start_date). Added two new source_urls: FNA waivers page and forms.in.gov RECORD OF IMPACT ATTENDANCE.
